creator: dougb@FreeBSD.org
text:
State Changed
From-To: open->closed


It's actually preferable for a variety of reasons to do certain types 
of patching in the Makefile, since it reduces repo bloat over time 
by avoiding the need to constantly re-generate common patches. 

Your suggestion of placing the conf file in its own directory is 
a good one however, although we hope that the vulnerability this guards 
against is no longer a problem.
